Our journey across Sri Lanka.

We spent three weeks driving across the country in a tuk-tuk. From chasing trains through the mountains to spending time with local fishermen, this adventure had a little bit of everything.
Short film dropping on YouTube tomorrow morning.

1 à 10: laquelle tu préfères? 🎞️
Dernière série photo de ce voyage à Komodo 🇮🇩
Je suis honnête avec toi, c’est une de mes plus belles expériences jusqu’ici dans ce magnifique pays: des paysages lunaires, des rencontres uniques et des moments de déconnexion totale 🤍
Je te partage un aperçu avec quelques anecdotes en caption sur chaque photo 📸

✨ C’est devenu notre mini-rituel du matin et aussi un de mes moments préférés: se lever tôt pour voir le sunrise sur le deck 

⛵️Pendant ce séjour, on a eu la chance d’être quasiment toujours seuls, c’était parfait pour déconnecter complètement. 

🐠 C’est une de mes rencontres préférées: les fameuses raies mantas. Je ne me rendais pas du tout compte à quel point elles sont gigantesques! 

🌿 J’avais aussi hâte de faire ce parc à ce moment de l’année parce que les paysages sont encore verts avant de tourner au orange. 

⚓️ Que ce soit sur l’eau ou sur terre, le mix était parfait entre découverte et repos. L’équipage nous a débarqué sur cette plage isolée où on a pu passer l’après-midi à bronzer. 

🐢 C’est toujours une experience unique de pouvoir nager avec une tortue: c’est incroyablement relaxant et on a eu la chance d’être avec elle un bon moment. 

⛰️ Voici un de mes points de vue préféré et aussi un des plus connus, l’île Padar. 

🌊 Fun fact: je pensais être malade en bateau mais au final ça a fait l’effet inverse, j’ai jamais aussi bien dormi! 

Notre dernier sunset, la photo parle d’elle-même je pense 🤍

Nothing beats a sunrise over the rice field in Ubud

#ubud #ubudbali #baliindonesia

Anyone else? 🎈

🇺🇸 Here’s the truth about buoyancy: not everyone floats the same 🙌🏼

It all depends on different factors: what wetsuit you’re wearing, how salty the water is, how deep underwater you are, how much air you’re holding, how big your lungs are, and of course: what your body composition is like 🙌🏼

In this case we were both full lungs, wearing no wetsuit, in the same salty water and at the same depth of about 8 meters. So the big difference is body composition 😌

👀 Fat floats more than muscle 👀

And yes it’s a generalization, but even the leanest women tend to float more than men because of the way their bodies are different (🍒🍑👀) 

Have you ever tested how much you float? 

•

🇮🇹 Anche voi? 🎈

Ecco la verità sulla galleggiabilità: non tutti stanno a galla nello stesso modo 🙌🏼

Dipende tutto da diversi fattori: la muta che indossi, quanto è salata l’acqua, a che profondità sei, quanta aria stai trattenendo, quanto sono grandi i tuoi polmoni, e ovviamente: la tua composizione corporea 🙌🏼

In questo caso eravamo entrambi a polmoni pieni, senza muta, nella stessa acqua salata e alla stessa profondità di 8 metri. Quindi la vera differenza è la composizione corporea ☺️

👀 Il grasso galleggia più del muscolo 👀

E sì è una generalizzazione, ma anche le ragazze più magre tendono a galleggiare di più degli uomini per come è fatto il corpo femminile (🍒🍑👀) 

Hai mai testato quanto galleggi?

•

Buoyancy • Freediving • Ocean Couple • Underwater Physics

Italy Together? 🇮🇹 Send this to someone you want to visit with ✨

Some places feel unreal. Positano is one of them.

Cliffside houses glowing in the sun, narrow streets leading down to the sea, and that easy Mediterranean pace that makes you forget what time it is.

Positano is a breathtaking, cliffside village on Italy’s Amalfi Coast. 

Famous for its pastel-colored houses, dramatic vertical layout, and stunning Mediterranean views, it features steep cobblestone streets, chic boutiques, and world-class seaside dining. It is easily reached via a ferry or taxi from nearby Naples or Sorrento.

📍 Positano, Amalfi Coast, Italy

#positano #italy #amalficoast #visititaly #italytravel travel coastalvibes beautifuldestinations travelgram europetravel shotoniphone

Send this to someone you’d love to explore Lake Como with ❤️🇮🇹

It’s one of Italy’s most beautiful lakes with many things to do. To fully enjoy it, I’d recommend at least to spend 3 days here, but you wouldn’t run out of things to do if you stay more! 

🏘️ Must see towns are Varenna, Bellagio, Menaggio and Como
💚 So many beautiful villas and gardens to see: Villa Monastero, Villa Carlotta, Villa del Balbianello, Villa Melzi and Villa Cipressi
🏡 The best place to stay is at the centre of the Lake, as you’ll be close to most sights. Menaggio, Varenna, Bellagio are among the best options, but some neighbouring towns might be a good alternative too.

🕥 When to visit? Best period is April/May or September, as it’s a bit less hot and less crowded. However, I love to visit in June as well, but in this case I’d recommend weekdays.
🚍 How to move around? Ferries are the best and fastest option. I’d recommend to purchase the tickets online and avoid to queue there, as it will likely take you forever.
Buses are a good alternative (on the Como to Menaggio route - C110 bus). It can take a little longer to move due to small road and traffic, but you can reach Lenno, Tremezzo and Menaggio from Como. (Tickets are made on the bus).

Are you planning to visit Lake Como soon? 💚

Growing up, I remember hearing the word “gay” used as an insult and believing there was something wrong with me.

I remember being as young as 10 and wishing on my birthdays that I could just be “normal.” Looking back at these photos now, I feel a lot of love for that kid.

I wish I could tell him there was never anything wrong with him.
I wish he didn’t have to carry shame for who he is.
I wish he knew just how full his life would become, and how much love and acceptance he would be met with.

Not everyone is met with that. And that’s why I’m sharing this.

Because right now, there’s still a kid sitting in a classroom, hearing those same words and wondering if there’s something wrong with them too.

This is for anyone who has ever felt different, for anyone still learning to love the parts of themselves they were taught to hide. You’re not alone. 

There was never anything wrong with you. 

Happy Pride 🏳️‍🌈
